title: Remove An AGV From The System Using The API Screen
summary: Remove a specified AGV from the system from the AGVs section of the API screen by entering the AGV ID and using the Remove AGV control. The source also states that if the AGV has an issue it must be physically removed from the system, and if it has a tote it must be taken to the hospital where the tote is removed and later recovered when put back in the system.
when_to_use: Use when a specified AGV must be removed from the system using its AGV ID from the AGVs section of the API screen, including recovery situations where the AGV has an issue and must be removed from service.
not_for: Do not use for adding an AGV back into the system; the source identifies Recover AGV as the add-back control.
Do not use when detailed hospital tote removal or tote recovery instructions are required beyond the brief source note in this section.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform AGV removal from the API screen, coordinate or perform physical AGV removal when the AGV has an issue, and ensure tote handling follows the source-described hospital process.

safety_notes: The source states that if an AGV has an issue, it must be physically removed from the system.
If the AGV has a tote, it must be taken to the hospital where the tote is removed.
This source section does not provide detailed physical handling or hospital safety steps; follow site-specific safety and handling procedures.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the System HMI API screen
AGVs section of the API screen
Physical access to remove the AGV from the system
Access to the hospital area for tote removal

access_required: Access to the AGVs section of the API screen
Access to the hospital area if tote removal is required
system_preconditions: The AGV ID of the AGV to be removed is known

visual_references.description: ACB API screen used to access system, AGV, and tote controls.
AGV API Controls screen showing the AGVs section and Remove AGV control.
Hospital HMI Add/Remove AGV screen relevant to AGV removal from service and return to service.
Tote API Controls screen referenced by related context for tote-related recovery actions.
Scanner/system screen related to adding a tote and confirming its return to the system.

steps.title: Open the AGVs section of the API screen
steps.purpose: Access the AGV controls used to remove an AGV from the system.
steps.instruction: Open the API screen and navigate to the AGVs section.
steps.expected_result: The AGVs section of the API screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV API Controls area is open and the Remove AGV control is available.
steps.failure_condition: The API screen or AGVs section cannot be accessed or the AGV controls are not visible.

actions.label: Open API and AGVs section
actions.exact_action: Open the AGVs section of the API screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: API screen entry point and overall ACB API interface.
AGVs section of the API screen and the Remove AGV control.

stop_or_escalate_if: The API screen cannot be opened.
The AGVs section is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users press API on the ACB System screen to access this screen. From this ACB API interface, the OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes can be controlled.
Figure 4-12 shows AGV API Controls in the AGVs section of the API screen.

steps.title: Locate the Remove AGV control and AGV ID field
steps.purpose: Identify the specific control and input needed to remove the target AGV.
steps.instruction: In the AGVs section, locate the Remove AGV control and the AGV ID entry field.
steps.expected_result: The Remove AGV control and AGV ID field are identified and ready for use.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can clearly identify where to enter the AGV ID and where to trigger removal.
steps.failure_condition: The Remove AGV control or AGV ID field cannot be identified.

actions.label: Find remove controls
actions.exact_action: Locate the Remove AGV control and the AGV ID entry field.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Remove AGV control and AGV ID entry area in the AGV API Controls screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Remove AGV control is missing.
The AGV ID entry field is not present or not usable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remove AGV: An AGV can be removed from the system by entering the AGV ID.

steps.title: Enter the AGV ID
steps.purpose: Specify the AGV that is to be removed from the system.
steps.instruction: Enter the AGV ID for the AGV to be removed.
steps.expected_result: The target AGV ID is entered in the AGV ID field.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended AGV ID is present in the entry field.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV ID is unknown, cannot be entered, or appears incorrect.

actions.label: Enter AGV ID
actions.exact_action: Enter the AGV ID for the AGV to be removed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV ID entry field used with Remove AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct AGV ID is not known.
The AGV ID cannot be entered.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remove AGV: An AGV can be removed from the system by entering the AGV ID.

steps.title: Use Remove AGV to remove the AGV from the system
steps.purpose: Execute the system-side removal of the specified AGV.
steps.instruction: Use the Remove AGV control to remove that AGV from the system.
steps.expected_result: The specified AGV is removed from the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is no longer in service in the system after the removal action.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV remains in the system or the removal action cannot be completed.

actions.label: Remove AGV
actions.exact_action: Use the Remove AGV control to remove that AGV from the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Remove AGV control in the AGV API Controls screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV is not removed from the system after using Remove AGV.
The system does not accept the removal action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remove AGV: An AGV can be removed from the system by entering the AGV ID.

steps.title: Physically remove the AGV if it has an issue
steps.purpose: Remove a problem AGV from the physical system as required by the source.
steps.instruction: If the AGV has an issue, physically remove the AGV from the system.
steps.expected_result: The issue-affected AGV is physically removed from the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is no longer physically present in the active system where it can affect operation.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be physically removed or remains in place despite having an issue.

actions.label: Physically remove AGV
actions.exact_action: If the AGV has an issue, physically remove the AGV from the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI Add/Remove AGV screen associated with removing an AGV from service.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be physically removed from the system.
Physical removal requires additional undocumented handling steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If an AGV has an issue, it must be physically removed from the system.
This screen is used to add a removed AGV back into the system or remove an AGV from the system for maintenance or when it is not needed for the sortation shift.

steps.title: Take a tote-carrying AGV to the hospital and remove the tote
steps.purpose: Handle tote removal from an AGV that is being removed from service.
steps.instruction: If the AGV has a tote, take the AGV to the hospital and remove the tote there.
steps.expected_result: The tote is removed from the AGV at the hospital.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV reaches the hospital process point and the tote is removed there.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be taken to the hospital or the tote cannot be removed.

actions.label: Move AGV to hospital
actions.exact_action: If the AGV has a tote, take the AGV to the hospital.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI Add/Remove AGV screen for AGV handling at the hospital station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be taken to the hospital.
The tote cannot be removed at the hospital.
Detailed hospital handling instructions are needed but not available in this source section.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the AGV has a tote, it must be taken to the hospital where the tote is removed.

steps.title: Return the tote to the system for recovery
steps.purpose: Restore the removed tote to the system so it can be recovered.
steps.instruction: Return the tote to the system so it can be recovered when it is put back in the system.
steps.expected_result: The tote is returned for recovery back into the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is put back into the system and is available for recovery.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be returned to the system or recovery cannot be completed.

actions.label: Return tote to system
actions.exact_action: Return the tote to the system so it can be recovered when it is put back in the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add Tote or confirmation-related screen associated with returning a tote into the system.
Tote API Controls area relevant to tote-related system actions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote cannot be returned to the system.
The tote recovery process cannot be completed with available documented procedures.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The tote is then "recovered" when it is put back in the system.
Navigate to the Add Tote screen, scan the tote barcode, verify the tote ID is displayed correctly, and press Confirm & Execute so the AGV takes the tote back into the system.

success_criteria: The specified AGV is removed from the system using the AGV ID.
If the AGV had an issue, it is physically removed from the system.
If the AGV had a tote, the tote is removed at the hospital and returned to the system for recovery.
healthy_conditions: The AGVs section of the API screen is accessible.
The Remove AGV control and AGV ID field are available.
The target AGV is removed from the system after the removal action.
Any tote on the AGV is removed through the hospital process and later returned to the system.
failure_conditions: The API screen or AGVs section cannot be accessed.
The Remove AGV control or AGV ID field cannot be used.
The AGV is not removed from the system after the removal action.
A problem AGV cannot be physically removed from the system.
A tote on the AGV cannot be removed at the hospital or cannot be returned for recovery.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the AGV cannot be removed from the system using the API screen.
Escalate if the AGV has an issue and cannot be physically removed from the system.
Escalate if tote removal at the hospital cannot be completed.
Escalate if tote recovery back into the system cannot be completed with available documented procedures.
Escalate when additional physical handling, confirmation prompts, or hospital workflow details are required but not provided in this source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: API access point from the ACB System screen.
AGV API Controls including Remove AGV and AGV ID entry.
Hospital HMI screen for removing an AGV from service.
Tote API Controls relevant to tote-related recovery context.
Tote return/add flow related to putting a tote back into the system.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Remove AGV: An AGV can be removed from the system by entering the AGV ID. If an AGV has an issue, it must be physically removed from the system. If the AGV has a tote, it must be taken to the hospital where the tote is removed. The tote is then "recovered" when it is put back in the system.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact button press order beyond entering the AGV ID and using Remove AGV.
The source does not provide confirmation prompts, validation messages, or post-removal verification details.
The source does not provide detailed physical removal instructions for the AGV.
The source does not provide detailed hospital tote removal workflow in this section.
The source does not provide a complete tote recovery procedure in this section.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
